name: Loops
description: Send transactional emails, fire product events and manage contacts in Loops (the modern product-email tool for SaaS) from any AI agent. 9 tools, Bearer-token auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the Loops v1 REST API (https://loops.so/docs/api-reference).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to https://app.loops.so → **Settings → API → Create API key**.
2. Pick the scope: Public API (recommended for most use) or higher.
3. Copy the key (prefixed). Set `LOOPS_API_KEY`.

**Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer ${LOOPS_API_KEY}`.

**Mental model**: Loops is event-driven. You send `events` (like 'user.signed_up', 'subscription.upgraded'), and those events trigger `loops` (the email automations you build in the UI). For transactional sends (password reset, magic link), use `loops_send_transactional_email` with a pre-built transactional template ID.

**Contacts**: identified by `email` (primary key). Use `userGroup` to bucket contacts (free / pro / churned). Custom properties via the `properties` object.

**Transactional templates**: built in the Loops UI under **Transactional**. Each has a `transactionalId` (visible after publishing). Variables in the template (`{{firstName}}`) are filled from the `dataVariables` map on send.

**Idempotency**: events can include an `eventName` + `idempotencyKey` — duplicate calls with the same idempotency key within 24h are deduped.

**Rate limits**: 10 req/sec per API key for sends, higher for reads. On 429, back off.

**Out of scope here**: campaign management (Loops campaigns are visual UI-only), audience filters, attribution reports.

tools.name: loops_test_api_key
tools.description: Verify the API key works and return account info (team name, type). Health check.

tools.name: loops_find_contact
tools.description: Find a contact by email or userId. Returns the contact object with email, firstName, lastName, userGroup, userId, custom properties, subscribed flag.

email.description: Email to look up. Either email or userId required.

tools.name: loops_create_contact
tools.description: Create a new contact. Required: email. Errors if email already exists (use loops_update_contact or loops_upsert_contact).

subscribed.description: Subscribed to marketing emails. Default true.

mailingLists.description: Map of mailing list IDs → boolean (subscribed?).

tools.name: loops_update_contact
tools.description: Update an existing contact's fields. Errors if contact doesn't exist (use loops_upsert_contact for upsert).

tools.name: loops_delete_contact
tools.description: Permanently delete a contact. GDPR-friendly. Irreversible.

tools.name: loops_send_event
tools.description: Send a product/behavioral event to trigger a Loops automation. Required: email (or userId) + eventName. Any eventProperties become merge variables in the triggered emails.

email.description: Contact email. Either email or userId required.

eventName.description: Event name matching a Loops trigger (e.g. 'subscription.upgraded').

contactProperties.description: Properties to also update on the contact (upsert).

eventProperties.description: Event-specific properties — usable as {{eventProperties.X}} in triggered emails.

mailingLists.description: Update list memberships in same call.

tools.name: loops_send_transactional_email
tools.description: Send a transactional email using a pre-built Loops transactional template. The `transactionalId` comes from the Loops UI. Variables in the template are filled from `dataVariables`.

transactionalId.description: ID of the published transactional template (from Loops UI).

addToAudience.description: If true, also create the recipient as a contact if missing. Default false.

attachments.description: Array of {filename, contentType, data (base64)} attachments. Optional.

tools.name: loops_list_mailing_lists
tools.description: List mailing lists configured on the account. Returns each list's id, name, description, isPublic, createdAt.

tools.name: loops_list_custom_fields
tools.description: List custom contact properties defined on the account, with their data types. Required to know which keys are valid in `contactProperties` / contact updates.
